description: You are an infrastructure engineer automating the first step of a provisioning workflow. In the home directory (/home/user) create a configuration directory named provisioning (if it is already present, leave any existing contents untouched). Inside that directory, create (or overwrite if it already exists) a file named infra.env whose contents are exactly the two lines shown below—no extra blank lines, no leading/trailing spaces, and each line must end with the Unix newline character \n: Line 1: APP_ENV=production Line 2: MAX_CONNECTIONS=200 The automated grader will check that: 1. The directory /home/user/provisioning exists. 2. The file /home/user/provisioning/infra.env exists. 3. The file’s byte-for-byte contents are: APP_ENV=production\nMAX_CONNECTIONS=200\n (exactly 33 bytes). Perform this using a **single** shell command of your choice. Do not delete or modify anything else in the filesystem.
